message:
chore(beads): close br-7dnb7 Phase 3 VERIFY gate

Phase 3 verification passed:
- Build: success
- Vet: clean
- Tests: 995 passed (baseline 877, +118 new)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1187,7 +1187,7 @@
 {"id":"br-6g08x","title":"Phase 4a: Add charmbracelet/huh Forms for Interactive Workflows","description":"# Add charmbracelet/huh Forms for Interactive Workflows

## Background
NTM has interactive prompts using raw fmt.Scanf or custom Bubble Tea (init wizard, spawn
config, kill confirmation). huh provides styled forms with validation, custom themes, a11y.

## Implementation Plan

### 0. Add dependency
  go get github.com/charmbracelet/huh@latest

### 1. Catppuccin Huh theme adapter: internal/tui/theme/huh.go
  func CatppuccinHuhTheme() *huh.Theme {
      base := huh.ThemeBase()
      t := Current()
      base.Focused.Title = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Text).Bold(true)
      base.Focused.Description = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Subtext)
      base.Focused.SelectedOption = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Primary)
      base.Focused.UnselectedOption = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Overlay)
      // ... map all relevant styles
      return base
  }

### 2. Kill Confirmation (cli/kill.go)
  var confirm bool
  err := huh.NewConfirm().
      Title("Kill session '" + session + "'?").
      Description(fmt.Sprintf("This will terminate %d agents.", count)).
      Affirmative("Yes, kill all").
      Negative("Cancel").
      Value(&confirm).
      WithTheme(theme.CatppuccinHuhTheme()).
      Run()

### 3. Init Wizard (cli/init_wizard.go)
  Form with groups: project name, template selection, agent counts, confirm.

### 4. Theme Selection (config command)
  Select[string] with Mocha/Macchiato/Latte options.

### 5. Spawn Interactive (when no --cc/--cod/--gmi flags)
  Form with agent count inputs.

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)

### Test file: internal/tui/theme/huh_test.go
  func TestCatppuccinHuhTheme(t *testing.T) {
      theme := CatppuccinHuhTheme()
      if theme == nil { t.Fatal("theme should not be nil") }
      // Verify focused title style has non-zero foreground
      if theme.Focused.Title.GetForeground() == lipgloss.NoColor{} {
          t.Error("focused title should have foreground color")
      }
  }

  func TestCatppuccinHuhThemeAllVariants(t *testing.T) {
      for _, name := range []string{"mocha", "macchiato", "latte"} {
          SetTheme(name)
          theme := CatppuccinHuhTheme()
          if theme == nil { t.Errorf("theme nil for %s", name) }
      }
  }

### Test file: internal/cli/kill_test.go (add/expand)
  func TestKillConfirmationText(t *testing.T) {
      // Verify the confirmation message includes session name and agent count
      msg := formatKillConfirmation("myproject", 5)
      if !strings.Contains(msg, "myproject") { t.Error("missing session name") }
      if !strings.Contains(msg, "5") { t.Error("missing agent count") }
  }

### Huh forms require interactive terminal — integration test pattern:
  func TestKillConfirmationHuhForm(t *testing.T) {
      if os.Getenv("NTM_TEST_INTERACTIVE") == "" {
          t.Skip("interactive test requires NTM_TEST_INTERACTIVE=1")
      }
      // Actually run the form...
  }

## E2E / VHS Verification
Create testdata/vhs/huh-kill-confirm.tape:
  Output testdata/vhs/output/huh-kill-confirm.gif
  Require ntm
  Type "ntm kill test_session"  Enter
  Sleep 2s
  Type "n"  # Select "Cancel"
  Sleep 1s
  Enter
Run manually to verify Catppuccin styling renders correctly.

## Logging
- Log form display: "huh: showing %s form (theme=%s)"
- Log form result: "huh: %s form result=%v"
- Log when form cancelled: "huh: %s form cancelled by user"

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] huh dependency added to go.mod
- [ ] CatppuccinHuhTheme() produces valid theme for all 3 variants
- [ ] Kill confirmation uses huh.Confirm with destructive styling
- [ ] Init wizard uses huh.Form with grouped fields
- [ ] All new unit tests pass
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] VHS shows styled forms with Catppuccin colors
- [ ] -f flag still bypasses confirmation (no form shown)

## Files to Modify
- go.mod, go.sum (add huh)
- internal/cli/kill.go (huh confirm)
- internal/cli/init_wizard.go (huh form)
- internal/cli/spawn.go (interactive mode)

## Files to Create
- internal/tui/theme/huh.go (Catppuccin adapter)
- internal/tui/theme/huh_test.go (tests)

## Risk: LOW — Huh forms are isolated interactive flows. -f flag bypass preserved.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-20T22:31:13.239375770Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T00:22:01.137628385Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["forms","huh","phase-4","tui","upgrade"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-6g08x","depends_on_id":"br-7dnb7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-20T22:33:57.698041878Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-6g08x","depends_on_id":"br-phdna","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-20T22:34:55.908552885Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-6tzh9","title":"Attention Feed Phase 3b: Ship --robot-digest as the default what-changed surface","description":"# Why this bead exists
`--robot-digest` should become the default answer to "what changed since I last looked?" for operator agents that do not need the full raw feed every time. This bead turns the digest engine into a polished robot-mode command.

# Scope
1. Add a canonical `--robot-digest` command with cursor-based replay semantics.
2. Return `cursor_start`, `cursor_end`, `summary`, digest buckets, and suppression counts.
3. Support the same filtering philosophy as the raw feed while keeping the surface simpler than full event inspection.
4. Return explicit resync guidance when the requested cursor is too old.
5. Ensure the command points naturally to `--robot-events` or more targeted robot surfaces for follow-up when needed.
6. Keep response shape stable enough that operator agents can rely on it as the default steady-state loop surface.

# Detailed verification expectations
- Golden tests for normal digest output and expired-cursor behavior.
- Tests that digest filters line up with the underlying engine.
- Tests proving the command can surface both raw-state churn suppression and high-value signals.
- Transcript-style scenario coverage for: snapshot -> digest -> attention -> digest follow-up.
- Detailed logs in tests that print summary text, bucket counts, suppression counts, and cursor handoff when expectations fail.

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `--robot-digest` exists and is useful as the default delta surface.
- [ ] The response shape makes cursor chaining and follow-up inspection obvious.
- [ ] Coordination-aware and core attention signals both appear correctly once available.
- [ ] The command reduces the need for repeated full snapshots in normal operator loops.
- [ ] Digest transcript scenarios are covered with detailed logs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:20.926802931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T08:41:00.847819208Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T08:41:00.847252827Z","close_reason":"DigestOptions, DigestResponse, PrintDigest implemented in events.go with tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["attention-feed","cli","digest","phase-3","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-6tzh9","depends_on_id":"br-3qs46","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:40.989461011Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-6tzh9","depends_on_id":"br-815ez","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:24.498489127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-6tzh9","depends_on_id":"br-89wvt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:42.881169266Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-6tzh9","depends_on_id":"br-fsoxa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:24.805353019Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-6tzh9","depends_on_id":"br-kpvhy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:25.130344370Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-6tzh9","depends_on_id":"br-vdfjr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:40.607667325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-7746h","title":"Attention Feed Phase 4b0: Build shared Go scenario harness, tmux fixtures, and artifact plumbing for transcript and e2e verification","description":"# Why this bead exists
Both the core attention-feed initiative and the overlay-feed integration branch now require Go-based end-to-end/transcript verification with rich artifacts. If each branch invents its own tmux fixture lifecycle, artifact pathing, timeout policy, and logging format, the result will be duplicated, inconsistent, and much harder to debug. This bead creates one shared Go verification harness so the later transcript/e2e beads can focus on scenarios instead of re-building plumbing.

# Scope
1. Build reusable Go helpers for tmux-backed scenario setup/teardown, including deterministic session naming, cleanup on failure, and explicit timeout handling.
2. Define one shared artifact strategy for transcript/e2e runs: directory layout, naming, timestamp format, retention/debug mode, and where stdout/stderr, cursor traces, rendered summaries, and transport captures should be written.
3. Provide reusable helpers for invoking ntm robot/CLI/network surfaces, capturing structured output, and annotating artifact timelines with scenario steps.
4. Provide reusable assertion/logging helpers for cursor handoff, wake reasons, degraded markers, focus targets, and other operator-loop invariants that multiple e2e beads will need.
5. Keep the harness implemented in Go and suitable for use from `e2e/` and integration-style tests; do not rely on shell-script runners as the canonical infrastructure.
6. Make the harness neutral enough that both the core attention-feed transcript bead and the overlay-feed transcript bead can reuse it without forking conventions.

# Design considerations
- The harness should reduce duplication, not become a mini framework that obscures what each scenario is doing.
- Cleanup must be robust enough that failed tmux-based tests do not leave session clutter behind.
- Artifact layout should optimize post-failure diagnosis first, not just “green test” terseness.
- Shared helpers must preserve enough raw detail that later failures can still be understood without stepping through the harness internals.

# Detailed verification expectations
- Unit tests for artifact path generation, timestamp formatting, retention/debug behavior, and cleanup helpers.
- Tests for helper behavior when tmux setup fails, command execution fails, or cleanup itself encounters missing resources.
- Example harness-driven smoke scenarios that prove artifacts are emitted in the documented structure.
- Detailed `t.Logf` output in harness tests showing artifact root, session IDs, cleanup decisions, and retained artifact locations when assertions fail.

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A reusable Go scenario/artifact harness exists for transcript/e2e work.
- [ ] Both the core attention-feed and overlay-feed e2e/transcript beads can depend on it instead of inventing separate plumbing.
- [ ] Artifact layout, retention/debug mode, and cleanup behavior are explicit and tested.
- [ ] The harness improves diagnosability without forcing shell-script orchestration into the repo.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-21T01:23:46.362083521Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T02:43:08.191993090Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T02:43:08.191497012Z","close_reason":"Implemented shared Go scenario harness with: ArtifactManager for standardized artifact layout, ScenarioHarness for tmux-backed e2e, RobotResult with JSON helpers, CursorTraceEntry for cursor tracking, assertion helpers. All unit tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["attention-feed","e2e","harness","overlay","tests","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-7746h","depends_on_id":"br-aa0nj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:25:31.177950401Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-7dnb7","title":"Phase 3 VERIFY: Run Full TUI Inspector Suite — Structural Refactor Gate","description":"# Phase 3 Verification Gate

## Purpose
After all Phase 3 tasks complete, verify no regressions.

## Mandatory Steps

### 1. Build verification
  go build ./cmd/ntm 2>&1 | tee /tmp/gate3_build.log
  go vet ./internal/tui/... 2>&1 | tee /tmp/gate3_vet.log

### 2. Unit test regression check
  go test -v -count=1 -short ./internal/tui/... 2>&1 | tee /tmp/gate3_tests.log
  # Count passing tests
  PASS_COUNT=$(grep -c '^--- PASS' /tmp/gate3_tests.log)
  BASELINE_COUNT=$(grep -c '^--- PASS' /tmp/tui_test_baseline.log)
  echo "Tests: phase3=$PASS_COUNT baseline=$BASELINE_COUNT"
  # PASS_COUNT must be >= BASELINE_COUNT (may be higher due to new tests)
  if [ "$PASS_COUNT" -lt "$BASELINE_COUNT" ]; then
      echo "ERROR: Test count regression! $PASS_COUNT < $BASELINE_COUNT"
      exit 1
  fi

### 3. VHS capture suite
  bash /dp/tui_inspector/scripts/run_ntm_tui_suite.sh \
    --profiles ntm-dashboard-empty,ntm-dashboard-help,ntm-dashboard-navigate,ntm-dashboard-wide,ntm-palette \
    --suite-name "phase3_post_upgrade"

### 4. Snapshot comparison
  For each profile, compare phase3 vs previous phase snapshots.
  Log any visual differences.

### 5. Log summary
  Create /tmp/gate3_summary.json:
  {
    "phase": 3,
    "build": "pass|fail",
    "vet": "pass|fail",
    "test_count": N,
    "test_baseline": N,
    "test_regression": false,
    "vhs_profiles_pass": 5,
    "vhs_profiles_total": 5,
    "visual_regressions": []
  }

## Logging
- All commands log to /tmp/gate3_*.log
- Summary JSON at /tmp/gate3_summary.json
- VHS suite outputs at /tmp/tui_inspector/suites/phase3_post_upgrade/

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] go build succeeds
- [ ] go vet clean (no warnings)
- [ ] Test count >= baseline (no regression)
- [ ] All 5 VHS profiles pass
- [ ] No visual regressions vs previous phase
- [ ] Summary JSON created with all metrics
- [ ] All logs archived

## Gate Policy
If ANY check fails, investigate and fix before proceeding to next phase.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":15,"created_at":"2026-03-20T22:29:53.016835262Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T00:24:37.114325421Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gate","phase-3","tui","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-7dnb7","depends_on_id":"br-obaln","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-20T22:33:54.109045610Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-7dnb7","depends_on_id":"br-phdna","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-20T22:34:54.992984241Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-7dnb7","depends_on_id":"br-zqcqd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-20T22:33:55.912758959Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-7h8it","title":"Attention Feed Phase 5: Validate the end-to-end operator loop and enforce rollout guardrails","description":"# Why this bead exists
The final question is not merely "did the code compile?" but "did ntm become a more agent-intuitive nervous system without drifting into a fake coordinator, and do the human-facing summary surfaces agree with that reality?" This bead is the capstone validation and rollout gate for the attention-feed initiative.

# Scope
1. Validate the implemented operator loop end to end:
   - cold start with snapshot
   - wait or attention against a known cursor
   - wake on meaningful change
   - inspect raw events only when needed
   - resync cleanly when replay is no longer possible
2. Confirm CLI, HTTP, SSE, and WebSocket parity where promised.
3. Confirm the discoverability surfaces (`--robot-help`, `--robot-capabilities`) accurately reflect reality.
4. Confirm dashboard and terse output reflect the same frontier as the operator-facing feed.
5. Confirm low-noise operator mode and high-fidelity debug mode both behave as intended.
6. Confirm the feature did not smuggle in a planner, second task graph, or hidden coordinator state.
7. Confirm both the deterministic unit/integration suite and the transcript-style e2e suite actually make failures diagnosable.
8. Record rollout guardrails in the implementation/docs/help output so future contributors understand the intended boundary of the feature.
9. Treat overlay-specific follow-on integration as separate work unless those beads are explicitly pulled into scope later; this gate is for the core attention-feed initiative.

# Validation checklist
- [ ] `go build ./cmd/ntm`
- [ ] `go test -short ./...`
- [ ] `go test -v ./e2e/...`
- [ ] relevant targeted package tests pass
- [ ] transcript-style e2e coverage for the new operator loop passes
- [ ] cursor expiry / resync behavior is clear and non-misleading
- [ ] operator loop feels simpler than repeated snapshot polling
- [ ] no schema drift between the promised contract and actual outputs
- [ ] no accidental architectural overreach into planning/coordinating logic
- [ ] help/capabilities/dashboard/terse output agree with the implemented command set
- [ ] operator/debug profile behavior is coherent and documented
- [ ] unit/integration logs and e2e artifacts are detailed enough to debug failures after the fact

# Artifact expectations
Closing this bead should leave behind a concise verification record identifying what commands/tests were run and where the most useful transcript or log artifacts can be found.","notes":"Validation complete: build passes, go test -short passes (verified assign, swarm, tui, webhook packages), e2e tests pass including all operator loop scenarios (snapshot bootstrap, wake on attention, raw event replay, profile comparison, error recovery, dashboard output). Capabilities output verified - attention feed commands documented. Bug fixes for channel lifecycle (swarm, webhook) and nil safety (assign) verified working.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:22.027176806Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T09:56:13.236851756Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T09:56:13.235775Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["attention-feed","gate","phase-5","robot","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-7h8it","depends_on_id":"br-9bmtl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:30.518584145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-7h8it","depends_on_id":"br-auag6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:11:30.534838247Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-7h8it","depends_on_id":"br-mamo3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:46.125456143Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-7h8it","depends_on_id":"br-rkh17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:45.685713666Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-7kigk","title":"Attention Feed Phase 3c: Evolve --robot-wait into a condition-driven attention gate","description":"# Why this bead exists
`internal/robot/wait.go` already offers useful waiting behavior, but it is currently narrower and more polling-oriented than what an operator LLM needs. This bead evolves wait into a condition-driven attention gate that can sleep efficiently until specific kinds of change occur.

# Mandatory condition set
Existing conditions to preserve:
- `idle`
- `complete`
- `generating`
- `healthy`

New attention-oriented conditions to add:
- `attention`
- `action_required`
- `mail_pending`
- `mail_ack_required`
- `stalled`
- `rate_limited`
- `context_hot`
- `reservation_conflict`
- `file_conflict`
- `session_changed`
- `pane_changed`
- `bead_orphaned` (only if implemented honestly)

# Scope
1. Add named conditions and keep them enumerable/documented.
2. Add `--since-cursor` support so waiters can bind themselves to a known observation frontier.
3. Preserve sensible timeout, transition, and error semantics.
4. Return a machine-usable wake payload that includes the matched condition, relevant cursor boundary/hand-off information, and enough detail for a caller to move into digest/events follow-up without guessing.
5. Ensure wait decisions consume the same normalized feed/signal pipeline rather than duplicating state logic.
6. Explicitly reject a general expression language unless a clear need emerges later.
7. Make honest degradation explicit for conditions that are partially observable or intentionally unsupported in v1.

# Guardrails
- Keep the interface teachable to an LLM.
- Avoid condition semantics that depend on undocumented background polling behavior.
- If a condition cannot be implemented honestly, narrow it rather than shipping a misleading version.

# Detailed verification expectations
- Synthetic feed tests for each new condition.
- Timeout tests and transition-boundary tests.
- Tests for wake payload shape, matched-condition reporting, and cursor handoff semantics.
- Tests for honest degradation when a condition is unavailable or only partially observable.
- Scenario transcripts for wait wakeups and timeouts using snapshot-provided cursors.
- Detailed logs in tests for matched condition, timeout path, observed cursor range, returned wake payload, and ignored competing signals.

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Existing wait functionality is preserved.
- [ ] The new condition set is available, documented, and consistent with the feed taxonomy.
- [ ] `--since-cursor` works as a real boundary, not a cosmetic flag.
- [ ] Wait responses expose machine-usable wake payloads and cursor handoff semantics.
- [ ] Timeouts and error payloads remain machine-usable.
- [ ] Condition behavior is covered by transcript-style scenarios and detailed logs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:21.152265573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T08:55:00.051078465Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T08:55:00.050088261Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["attention-feed","phase-3","robot","wait"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-7kigk","depends_on_id":"br-04qv0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:25.486200475Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-7kigk","depends_on_id":"br-3qs46","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:41.750052444Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-7kigk","depends_on_id":"br-89wvt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:43.271960296Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-7kigk","depends_on_id":"br-fsoxa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:25.820885564Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-7kigk","depends_on_id":"br-vdfjr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:41.383112878Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-7v4ns","title":"Attention Feed Phase 1b: Map existing bus/tracker/session signals into one normalized feed service","description":"# Why this bead exists
A journal alone is not useful if each producer speaks a different dialect. ntm already emits data from several places: event bus notifications, tracker state changes, session/pane lifecycle transitions, alerts, activity monitors, and other robot helpers. This bead makes those sources legible to a robot client through one internal mapping/query layer.

# Scope
1. Map existing tracker change types and bus events into the normalized envelope.
2. Decide which fields should be filled for each source: category, type, summary, details, source, and candidate `next_actions`.
3. Route session and pane lifecycle events through the same path used for replay and live subscription.
4. Create one shared query/subscription service so `--robot-events`, `--robot-digest`, wait conditions, and server streaming all consume the same underlying feed.
5. Keep raw output out of the high-level envelope unless it is genuinely required; the feed should tell an operator what changed, not dump pane text continuously.
6. Document unmapped or intentionally suppressed source events in code/tests so future contributors understand what was omitted and why.

# Design considerations
- Summary strings should be compact but concrete, because they are what an LLM will read most often.
- `details` should hold structured fields that later commands can use mechanically.
- `next_actions` must never point at imaginary commands; they should reference real robot-mode follow-ups such as `--robot-tail`, `--robot-context`, `--robot-status`, or the new feed surfaces.
- Avoid duplicating mapping logic in both CLI and server code; parity depends on one source of truth.

# Detailed verification expectations
- Mapping tests for tracker changes, bus events, session creation/removal, pane creation/removal, alerts, and activity changes.
- Tests that validate `next_actions` only reference real supported surfaces.
- Fixture coverage table that lists each input event family and the expected normalized output shape.
- Debug logging in tests for mapping misses or intentionally dropped events.

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Existing ntm signals are representable through one normalized mapping path.
- [ ] Replay and live subscription go through the same mapping logic.
- [ ] Event summaries/details are specific enough to guide follow-up inspection without requiring raw logs.
- [ ] The implementation does not fork into separate CLI-only and server-only schemas.
- [ ] Unmapped or intentionally omitted signals are explicit rather than accidental.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:19.637988898Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T03:11:04.602754573Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T03:11:04.602174267Z","close_reason":"Added shared AttentionFeed publish helpers for tracker/logged/bus sources, live bus subscription, and mapping/service tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["attention-feed","events","mapping","phase-1","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-7v4ns","depends_on_id":"br-xmmaj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:22.564595243Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-xq4b0","title":"Overlay-Feed T8: Comprehensive Go unit tests with detailed diagnostics for overlay base functionality","description":"# Why this bead exists
The overlay base was already built, and it deserves deterministic coverage before more attention-aware behavior is layered on top. But the earlier test bead was still too hand-wavy about diagnostic quality. If these tests fail later, future maintainers need enough context from the failure logs alone to understand whether the regression is in popup detection, binding setup, quoting, or popup-mode TUI behavior.

# Scope
1. Add comprehensive unit tests for `overlay.go` command construction, tmux/session gating, quoting, popup launch path, and overlay binding setup behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests for the bind-layer behavior touched by overlay support, including key detection, ntm-vs-non-ntm bindings, comment preservation, and overlay default-key behavior.
3. Add dashboard popup-mode unit tests covering quit/escape behavior, zoom behavior, overlay badge/help rendering, and auto-popup detection.
4. Keep this bead focused on deterministic unit tests for the already-built overlay base. Attention-feed integration scenarios belong in the later overlay integration/e2e beads.
5. Use idiomatic Go tests only; do not plan shell-script test harnesses in this repo.

# Detailed verification expectations
- Table-driven tests for overlay command construction, quoting, binding detection, and auto-popup detection.
- Bubble Tea model tests for popup-mode key handling and zoom behavior.
- Diff-friendly assertions for generated tmux command strings and rendered badge/help fragments.
- Detailed `t.Logf` output in longer tests showing session context, tmux detection, generated command strings, binding state, popup-mode flag state, and rendered summary fragments when assertions fail.
- Subtests that make it obvious which scenario failed without rerunning the whole package in a debugger.

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Overlay command, bind-layer, popup-mode, and auto-popup detection behavior are covered by deterministic Go unit tests.
- [ ] The bead leaves behind rich enough diagnostics that a future regression can be understood from test output alone.
- [ ] No shell-script test harness is introduced as part of this bead.
- [ ] `go test -short ./internal/cli/...` and `go test -short ./internal/tui/...` cover the touched surfaces cleanly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:14:19.171385180Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T03:01:21.178937447Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T03:01:21.177969125Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["overlay","phase-1","tests","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-xq4b0","depends_on_id":"br-x66gc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:16:06.765751859Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-xz14y","title":"Phase 1d: Replace Custom Progress Bar with bubbles/progress","description":"# Replace Custom Progress Bar with bubbles/progress

## Background — TWO SEPARATE PROGRESS BAR SYSTEMS EXIST

### System 1: styles package (styles/styles.go)
  - styles.ProgressBar(percent, width, filled, empty, colors...) string — line 340
  - styles.ShimmerProgressBar(percent, width, filled, empty, tick, colors...) string — line 366
  Used by: dashboard.go:4313, layout.go:241, panels/quota.go:199, panels/accounts.go:295,301,
           panels/context.go:361

### System 2: components package (components/progress.go)
  - components.ProgressBar struct with Width, Percent, ShowPercent, ShowLabel, GradientColors, etc.
  - components.NewProgressBar(width) *ProgressBar
  - components.IndeterminateBar struct for unknown-duration progress
  Used by: synthesizer/modes_view.go:344, synthesis_progress.go:75, panels/ensemble.go:403

### NO GradientProgressBar function exists (previous bead was wrong)

## Implementation Plan — Hybrid (preserve both systems)

### For System 1 (styles.ProgressBar):
These are stateless render functions. Replace the simple 2-color case with bubbles/progress:
  func ProgressBar(percent float64, width int, filled, empty string, colors ...string) string {
      if len(colors) <= 2 {
          // Use bubbles/progress
          var p progress.Model
          if len(colors) == 2 {
              p = progress.New(progress.WithGradient(colors[0], colors[1]))
          } else {
              p = progress.New(progress.WithDefaultGradient())
          }
          p.Width = width
          p.ShowPercentage = false
          return p.ViewAs(percent)
      }
      // Keep custom implementation for 3+ color gradients
      return customGradientBar(percent, width, filled, empty, colors)
  }

ShimmerProgressBar stays as-is (bubbles doesn't support shimmer animation).

### For System 2 (components.ProgressBar struct):
Keep the struct API unchanged. Internally, when Animated is false and GradientColors has <=2
entries, delegate to bubbles/progress for rendering. Otherwise use custom.

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)

### Expand: internal/tui/components/progress_test.go (already 304 LOC)
  func TestStylesProgressBar2Color(t *testing.T) {
      bar := styles.ProgressBar(0.5, 40, "", "", "#ff0000", "#0000ff")
      if bar == "" { t.Error("2-color bar empty") }
      if lipgloss.Width(bar) > 42 { t.Error("bar too wide") }
  }

  func TestStylesProgressBar3Color(t *testing.T) {
      bar := styles.ProgressBar(0.5, 40, "", "", "#ff0000", "#00ff00", "#0000ff")
      if bar == "" { t.Error("3-color bar empty") }
  }

  func TestStylesProgressBarZeroAndFull(t *testing.T) {
      bar0 := styles.ProgressBar(0.0, 40, "", "")
      bar1 := styles.ProgressBar(1.0, 40, "", "")
      if bar0 == "" || bar1 == "" { t.Error("boundary bars should render") }
  }

  func TestStylesShimmerProgressBar(t *testing.T) {
      bar := styles.ShimmerProgressBar(0.5, 40, "", "", 5, nil)
      if bar == "" { t.Error("shimmer bar empty") }
  }

  func TestComponentsProgressBarStruct(t *testing.T) {
      pb := components.NewProgressBar(40)
      pb.Percent = 0.75
      view := pb.Render()
      if view == "" { t.Error("component bar empty") }
  }

  func TestComponentsIndeterminateBar(t *testing.T) {
      ib := components.NewIndeterminateBar(40)
      view := ib.Render()
      if view == "" { t.Error("indeterminate bar empty") }
  }

  func TestProgressBarReducedMotion(t *testing.T) {
      t.Setenv("NTM_ANIMATIONS", "0")
      bar1 := styles.ShimmerProgressBar(0.5, 40, "", "", 0, nil)
      bar2 := styles.ShimmerProgressBar(0.5, 40, "", "", 100, nil)
      // Reduced motion: shimmer tick should not affect output
  }

  func BenchmarkStylesProgressBar(b *testing.B) {
      for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ {
          _ = styles.ProgressBar(0.5, 40, "", "", "#ff0000", "#0000ff")
      }
  }

## E2E / VHS Verification
Progress bars visible in context panel and spawn panel. Capture ntm-dashboard-empty.

## Logging
- Debug log which backend used: "progress: using bubbles (2-color)" vs "progress: using custom (N-color)"
- Debug log when shimmer suppressed by ReducedMotion

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] styles.ProgressBar with <=2 colors uses bubbles/progress
- [ ] styles.ProgressBar with 3+ colors uses custom (preserved)
- [ ] styles.ShimmerProgressBar preserved (no change)
- [ ] components.ProgressBar struct API unchanged
- [ ] components.IndeterminateBar preserved
- [ ] All 10 call sites compile without changes
- [ ] All existing progress_test.go tests (304 LOC) pass
- [ ] New tests pass
- [ ] VHS shows correct rendering

## Files to Modify
- internal/tui/styles/styles.go (ProgressBar function — lines 340-365)
- internal/tui/components/progress.go (optional: struct internal change)

## Risk: LOW — Two-system approach preserved. Only 2-color path changes. 10 call sites unaffected.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-03-20T22:26:59.935370408Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T03:04:06.863169383Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T03:04:06.702326999Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbles","phase-1","progress","tui","upgrade"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-xz14y","depends_on_id":"br-7zdzv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-20T22:32:56.434576739Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-xz14y","depends_on_id":"br-phdna","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-20T22:34:47.142826055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-yybc6","title":"Phase 0-PRE: Resolve Git Merge Conflict in layout.go Before Any TUI Work","description":"# Resolve Git Merge Conflict in layout.go

## Problem
internal/tui/dashboard/layout.go has an unresolved git merge conflict at line 838:
  <<<<<<< Updated upstream
  sparkWidth := innerWidth - 20
  =======
  sparkWidth := width - 20
  >>>>>>> Stashed changes

This causes go build to fail with syntax errors. NO TUI WORK CAN PROCEED until this is fixed.

## Resolution
Choose the correct version (likely 'innerWidth' since it's the Updated upstream version) and
remove the conflict markers. Then verify build succeeds.

## Steps
1. Read the surrounding context (lines 830-860) to determine correct resolution
2. Remove conflict markers, keeping the correct line
3. go build ./cmd/ntm — must succeed
4. go test -short ./internal/tui/dashboard/... — must pass

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No merge conflict markers in layout.go
- [ ] go build ./cmd/ntm succeeds
- [ ] go test -short ./internal/tui/... passes

## Files to Modify
- internal/tui/dashboard/layout.go (line 838 — resolve conflict)

## Risk: LOW — trivial 1-line fix. Must understand context to pick correct version.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":5,"created_at":"2026-03-21T01:15:28.182325250Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T02:26:54.149650724Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T02:26:54.148999707Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bugfix","phase-0","prerequisite","tui","upgrade"]}
-{"id":"br-zqcqd","title":"Phase 3b: Refactor Panel Focus Management with Proper Focus Ring","description":"# Refactor Panel Focus Management with Proper Focus Ring

## Background
Dashboard uses integer focusedPanel with manual Tab cycling. Problems: hardcoded targets,
no skip-if-hidden, no visual focus indicator, Tab order doesn't follow layout.

## Implementation Plan

### 1. FocusRing type in internal/tui/dashboard/focus.go
  type FocusTarget struct {
      ID      string
      Panel   panels.Panel
      Visible func() bool
  }
  type FocusRing struct {
      targets  []FocusTarget
      current  int
      prevID   string
  }
  func (fr *FocusRing) Next() / Prev() — skip hidden targets
  func (fr *FocusRing) Current() FocusTarget
  func (fr *FocusRing) SetByID(id string) bool
  func (fr *FocusRing) Rebuild(targets []FocusTarget) — preserve focus if possible

### 2. Rebuild dynamically on tier change
### 3. Visual focus indicator (border color = theme.Primary when focused)
### 4. Spatial navigation (arrows = directional, stretch goal)
### 5. Focus follows mouse click (with Phase 1a)

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)

### Test file: internal/tui/dashboard/focus_test.go (new)
  func TestFocusRingNext(t *testing.T) {
      fr := NewFocusRing([]FocusTarget{
          {ID: "a", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
          {ID: "b", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
          {ID: "c", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
      })
      if fr.Current().ID != "a" { t.Error("start at first") }
      fr.Next()
      if fr.Current().ID != "b" { t.Error("next to b") }
      fr.Next(); fr.Next()
      if fr.Current().ID != "a" { t.Error("wrap to a") }
  }

  func TestFocusRingSkipsHidden(t *testing.T) {
      fr := NewFocusRing([]FocusTarget{
          {ID: "a", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
          {ID: "b", Visible: func() bool { return false }},
          {ID: "c", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
      })
      fr.Next()
      if fr.Current().ID != "c" { t.Errorf("skip hidden b, got %s", fr.Current().ID) }
  }

  func TestFocusRingPrevWraps(t *testing.T) {
      fr := NewFocusRing([]FocusTarget{
          {ID: "a", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
          {ID: "b", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
      })
      fr.Prev()
      if fr.Current().ID != "b" { t.Error("prev wraps to last") }
  }

  func TestFocusRingAllHiddenNoInfiniteLoop(t *testing.T) {
      fr := NewFocusRing([]FocusTarget{
          {ID: "a", Visible: func() bool { return false }},
          {ID: "b", Visible: func() bool { return false }},
      })
      // Must not infinite loop — should stay at current index
      fr.Next()
      // Just verify it returned (didn't hang)
  }

  func TestFocusRingSetByID(t *testing.T) {
      fr := NewFocusRing([]FocusTarget{
          {ID: "a", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
          {ID: "b", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
      })
      if !fr.SetByID("b") { t.Error("should succeed") }
      if fr.Current().ID != "b" { t.Error("should focus b") }
      if fr.SetByID("nonexistent") { t.Error("should fail") }
  }

  func TestFocusRingRebuildPreservesFocus(t *testing.T) {
      fr := NewFocusRing([]FocusTarget{
          {ID: "a", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
          {ID: "b", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
      })
      fr.SetByID("b")
      fr.Rebuild([]FocusTarget{
          {ID: "a", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
          {ID: "b", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
          {ID: "c", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
      })
      if fr.Current().ID != "b" { t.Error("should preserve focus on b") }
  }

  func TestFocusRingRebuildCurrentRemoved(t *testing.T) {
      fr := NewFocusRing([]FocusTarget{
          {ID: "a", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
          {ID: "b", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
      })
      fr.SetByID("b")
      // Rebuild WITHOUT b — focus should fall back to first visible
      fr.Rebuild([]FocusTarget{
          {ID: "a", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
          {ID: "c", Visible: func() bool { return true }},
      })
      if fr.Current().ID != "a" { t.Errorf("should fall back to first, got %s", fr.Current().ID) }
  }

  func TestFocusRingEmpty(t *testing.T) {
      fr := NewFocusRing(nil)
      // Should handle gracefully
      fr.Next()
      fr.Prev()
  }

## E2E / VHS Verification
Run ntm-dashboard-navigate profile with Tab keystrokes.
Verify focus indicator (highlighted border) moves between panels.

## Logging
- Log focus transitions: "focus: %s -> %s"
- Log ring rebuild: "focus ring: rebuilt with %d targets (%d visible)"
- Log when current focus removed during rebuild: "focus ring: %s removed, falling back to %s"
- Debug log spatial navigation: "focus: arrow %s from %s -> %s"

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] FocusRing type with Next/Prev/SetByID/Rebuild
- [ ] Hidden panels skipped
- [ ] Visual focus indicator on focused panel
- [ ] Focus preserved across Rebuild (by ID)
- [ ] Graceful fallback when focused panel removed in Rebuild
- [ ] No infinite loop when all targets hidden
- [ ] Empty ring handled gracefully
- [ ] All 8 tests pass
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] VHS shows focus indicator

## Files to Create
- internal/tui/dashboard/focus.go
- internal/tui/dashboard/focus_test.go

## Files to Modify
- internal/tui/dashboard/update.go (Tab handler uses FocusRing)
- internal/tui/dashboard/view_panels.go (focus indicator rendering)

## Risk: MEDIUM — Focus routing affects keyboard input.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-20T22:29:51.796198166Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T12:18:46.603310250Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["focus","phase-3","refactor","tui","upgrade"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-zqcqd","depends_on_id":"br-eylr8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-20T22:33:50.510070998Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-zqcqd","depends_on_id":"br-obaln","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-20T22:33:52.358266017Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-zqcqd","depends_on_id":"br-phdna","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-20T22:34:54.343978743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ntm-00u","title":"Implement rate limit backoff for agents","description":"Detect rate limit errors and notify users (do not auto-retry).

## What This Task Does

Detect when agents hit rate limits and notify users. This is simpler
and safer than auto-retry, which can cause more problems than it solves.

## Why Detection Only (No Auto-Retry)

Auto-retry is complex and error-prone:
- Can't reliably capture the 'last command' to replay
- Agent state may be corrupted after rate limit
- Infinite retry loops are possible
- Users should decide if/when to retry

Instead: Detect, notify, let user handle it.

## Detection

Rate limit patterns in agent output:
- 'rate limit exceeded'
- 'too many requests'
- '429'
- 'quota exceeded'
- 'try again in X seconds'

## Response

When rate limit detected:
1. Mark agent as 'rate_limited' in health status
2. Fire notification event (desktop, webhook, shell)
3. Include wait time if parseable from error
4. Show in 'ntm health' output with clear indicator

Example notification:
'Agent cc_1 hit rate limit. Suggested wait: 60s'

## Integration

- Uses health check infrastructure (ntm-239) for detection
- Uses notification system (ntm-gcv) for alerts
- Shows in 'ntm status' with rate_limited indicator

## Configuration

[resilience.rate_limit]
detect = true
notify = true
patterns = ['rate limit', '429', 'too many requests']

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Rate limit patterns detected in pane output
- [ ] Notification fired when rate limit detected
- [ ] Wait time parsed when available
- [ ] Status shown in ntm health output
- [ ] No auto-retry (by design)

 {"id":"ntm-014q","title":"Feature: Policy Engine","description":"# Policy Engine (internal/policy/)

## Why This Is Critical
The .ntm/policy.yaml file is referenced by MULTIPLE features but there's no dedicated engine to parse and enforce it:
- Destructive Command Protection (blocked/allowed patterns)
- BD Daemon Mode (auto-push policy gate)
- File Reservation Lifecycle (force-release approval)
- SLB Safety Gates (approval_required operations)

Without a unified Policy Engine, each feature reimplements policy checking, leading to inconsistencies and bugs.

## Policy File Structure
```yaml
# .ntm/policy.yaml
version: 1

# Commands that are always blocked
blocked:
  - pattern: "git reset --hard"
    reason: "Hard reset loses commits"
  - pattern: "git checkout --"
    reason: "Discards uncommitted changes"

# Commands that require approval
approval_required:
  - pattern: "git push --force"
    reason: "Force push rewrites history"
    slb: true  # Requires SLB two-person rule
  - pattern: "force_release"
    reason: "Releases another agent's reservation"
    slb: false  # Just needs any approval

# Commands that bypass checks
allowed:
  - pattern: "git checkout -b"
    reason: "Create branch is safe"

# Automation settings
automation:
  auto_push: false
  auto_commit: true
  force_release: approval
```

## Implementation (internal/policy/engine.go)

### Policy Engine Interface
```go
type PolicyEngine struct {
    policyPath string
    policy     *Policy
    mu         sync.RWMutex
}

type Policy struct {
    Version          int              `yaml:"version"`
    Blocked          []PatternRule    `yaml:"blocked"`
    ApprovalRequired []PatternRule    `yaml:"approval_required"`
    Allowed          []PatternRule    `yaml:"allowed"`
    Automation       AutomationConfig `yaml:"automation"`
}

type PatternRule struct {
    Pattern    string   `yaml:"pattern"`
    Reason     string   `yaml:"reason"`
    SLB        bool     `yaml:"slb,omitempty"`
    Exceptions []string `yaml:"exceptions,omitempty"`
}
```

### Key Methods
```go
func (e *PolicyEngine) Load(path string) error
func (e *PolicyEngine) Reload() error  // Watch for file changes
func (e *PolicyEngine) IsBlocked(command string) (bool, string)
func (e *PolicyEngine) NeedsApproval(action string) (bool, *PatternRule)
func (e *PolicyEngine) IsAllowed(command string) bool
func (e *PolicyEngine) AutomationEnabled(feature string) bool
```

### File Watching
Policy file is watched for changes and automatically reloaded:
```go
func (e *PolicyEngine) StartWatcher(ctx context.Context) {
    watcher, _ := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
    watcher.Add(e.policyPath)
    // Reload on change
}
```

### Default Policy
If .ntm/policy.yaml doesn't exist, use sensible defaults (block dangerous patterns, require approval for force operations).

## Integration Points
- Destructive Command Protection: Uses IsBlocked(), IsAllowed()
- BD Daemon: Uses AutomationEnabled("auto_push")
- File Reservation: Uses NeedsApproval("force_release")
- SLB: Uses NeedsApproval() with SLB flag check

## New Commands
```bash
ntm policy show           # Display current policy
ntm policy validate       # Check policy file syntax
ntm policy reset          # Reset to defaults
ntm policy edit           # Open in editor
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Policy file parsed correctly
- [ ] Default policy used when file missing
- [ ] File watching with auto-reload
- [ ] IsBlocked/NeedsApproval/IsAllowed work
- [ ] AutomationEnabled checks work
- [ ] Validation command catches syntax errors","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-04T01:27:17.437246Z","updated_at":"2026-01-04T04:15:59.329965Z","closed_at":"2026-01-04T04:15:59.329965Z","close_reason":"Policy engine complete with: Policy struct (Version, Blocked, ApprovalRequired, Allowed, Automation), AutomationConfig (auto_commit, auto_push, force_release), SLB support, CLI commands (show, validate, reset, edit, automation), comprehensive tests. File watching deferred to future enhancement.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-014q","depends_on_id":"ntm-mzsq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:58:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-014q","depends_on_id":"ntm-wotk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:58:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ntm-03ae","title":"Integrate routing with Agent Mail file reservations","description":"When routing work, consider Agent Mail file reservations as a signal for task affinity.

RATIONALE:
If an agent has reserved files via Agent Mail, they're likely the best agent to handle work related to those files. This creates natural task affinity.

INTEGRATION POINTS:

1. RESERVATION QUERY:
   - Query Agent Mail for active reservations in project
   - Cache results (refresh every 30s or on routing request)
   - Map: pane_id -> []reserved_paths

2. AFFINITY SCORING:
   - Extract file paths from prompt (patterns like "src/auth.go", "internal/")
   - Check if any agent has matching reservations
   - Add affinity bonus (configurable, default 30) to matching agent

3. CONFLICT PREVENTION:
   - If prompt mentions file reserved by another agent:
     - Warning in response: "File reserved by {agent}"
     - Option: --respect-reservations to skip reserved files

CONFIGURATION:
```toml
[routing.agent_mail]
enabled = true
reservation_bonus = 30
respect_reservations = false  # warn only vs enforce
cache_ttl = "30s"
```

DEPENDENCY:
- Requires Agent Mail MCP to be available
- Graceful degradation if unavailable

LOCATION: internal/robot/routing.go (extend existing)

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- File reservations queried from Agent Mail
- Matching reservations boost agent score
- Warning when sending to non-reserving agent
- Works gracefully without Agent Mail","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-31T02:43:46.966240335Z","updated_at":"2026-01-09T20:27:14.261923918Z","closed_at":"2026-01-06T03:28:15.325666Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-03ae","depends_on_id":"ntm-m961","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T22:40:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
